_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Saturday March 7th | 5:31

Bonjour Tremblant,

Conditions are changing fast today! Warm temperatures and rain are moving in throughout the day, so it's time to break out those waterproof spring layers. Strong winds are already picking up and will continue to intensify as the day progresses. Be extra cautious on all terrain, as the combination of rain and wind can make runs slippery and visibility challenging. Reduce your speed, stay aware of your surroundings, and know your limits.

Additionally, note that due to the changing conditions, a few trails will open progressively throughout the morning as our team assesses rain and wind impacts. Keep an eye on the trail map and check back regularly for updates. The good news is all 4 of our incredible terrain parks remain open today! From beginner-friendly features to more advanced setups, there's something for every freestyle rider. Grab your crew, lap your favorites, and make the most of it!

First Tracks Access to designated lifts on both North and South side as early as 7:45 a.m. Ski on designated North Side slopes at 8 a.m.

Alpine Touring & Snowshoeing Warming: temperatures combined with rain and strong winds create challenging conditions this morning. Trails will be wet and slippery. We strongly encourage you to exercise extreme caution and consider postponing your outing if conditions don't feel right.

Stay safe out there, respect the mountain, and have fun responsibly!

Mountain safety tip of the week: Slow down in reduced‑speed zones.
Reduced‑speed areas mean it’s time to slow down for your safety and the safety of others.
Remember, you’re sharing the slopes with other skiers and riders. Stay cautious at all times.
Some zones marked in yellow on the trail map require reduced speed. Please be considerate, and ski or ride slowly in these areas out of respect for the higher traffic.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Saturday brings spring conditions to Tremblant with warm temperatures, rain, and intensifying winds throughout the day. Several trails will open progressively as conditions are assessed. All 4 terrain parks remain open. First Tracks available on both sides at 7:45 a.m., North Side skiing at 8 a.m. Alpine touring and snowshoeing trails are wet and slippery; exercise caution or postpone. Wear waterproof layers and ski safely.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Bonjour Tremblant! Ce samedi apporte des conditions printanières à Tremblant avec un redoux, de la pluie et des vents s'intensifiant. Plusieurs pistes ouvriront donc progressivement selon les évaluations. Les 4 parcs restent ouverts! Les sentiers de randonnée et raquette sont mouillés et glissants; soyez prudents ou reportez. Portez des couches imperméables et skiez en sécurité.
